title: buildah: test rootless integration
body: Perform buildah's integration tests in a completely `rootless` environment.

comments.body: @cevich PTAL
This is still under WIP. @cevich @rhatdan I'll un-draft it when this is ready.
Nice start @flouthoc !

@nalind @rhatdan @giuseppe There are some tests which performs `mount` or something equivalent so we can't easily run them on rootless env without `unshare`, hence i'm skipping them for `rootless environment`.

However most of the tests run fine with `rootless` environment.
Comment them out for now, but it would be nice to see if we could do a `buildah unshare` before running these tests, when running in rootless mode.

> Comment them out for now, but it would be nice to see if we could do a buildah unshare before running these tests, when running in rootless mode.

I'm not the expert, but I respectfully disagree.  Bind-mounting is a pretty important feature.  Commenting them out means we're not testing them *at all*.  Adding a `if uid != 0` condition to do a `buildah unshare` or a `skip_if_rootless()` sounds pretty easy...but I'm not an expert on these tests.  I think at a minimum, if anything is commented out or skipped, an issue should be filed so we remember to address it later.

> > Comment them out for now, but it would be nice to see if we could do a buildah unshare before running these tests, when running in rootless mode.
> 
> I'm not the expert, but I respectfully disagree. Bind-mounting is a pretty important feature. Commenting them out means we're not testing them _at all_. Adding a `if uid != 0` condition to do a `buildah unshare` or a `skip_if_rootless()` sounds pretty easy...but I'm not an expert on these tests. I think at a minimum, if anything is commented out or skipped, an issue should be filed so we remember to address it later.

@cevich Ah i guess there is some misunderstanding `bind mount` tests will still work. Its just external `buildah mount` command which is causing issues not the `bind mount in Containerfile` or `containers`. We did similar skip and crafting for `mount` tests in rootless podman as well. But I don't think its that critical for rootless users so skipping them for now but we can enable it later.

Issue with `buildah unshare` is that it changes shell so all the tests using `buildah mount` would have to be executed separately inside a `buildah unshare` instance.

>Issue with buildah unshare is that it changes shell so all the tests using buildah mount would have to be executed separately inside a buildah unshare instance.

Oh okay, thanks for the explanation.  I would def. rec. opening an issue on it then, at least so 2 years from now nobody's left wondering "howcome all these tests are skipped?".

@cevich @rhatdan @nalind @TomSweeneyRedHat This is ready for review. 

Covers all the major `529` tests out of total `642` total integration tests.

Seems like a good start, and everyone gets an extra doughnut if they turn on one of the 113 disabled rootless tests.
LGTM

I feel like @edsantiago will win the most doughnuts.

@TomSweeneyRedHat @cevich @edsantiago @vrothberg @nalind @umohnani8 @ashley-cui PTAL

> Seems like a good start, and everyone gets an extra doughnut if they turn on one of the 113 disabled rootless tests. LGTM

Actually its fairly easy to test disabled tests as well we just need to do `./buildah unsare test_script` I added another test in the matrix for this.
@rhatdan @cevich Added `integration-rootless-unshare` that covers all the remaining tests as well except last `20` which is more of `root` user tests.

With several enormous caveats (see below), the following diff goes a long way toward enabling (some) rootless tests on my f35:
```diff
index 596abdc6..b94c1c7e 100644
--- a/tests/helpers.bash
+++ b/tests/helpers.bash
@@ -160,6 +160,31 @@ function podman() {
     command podman ${PODMAN_REGISTRY_OPTS} ${ROOTDIR_OPTS} "$@"
 }
 
+# Used in rootless
+function run_unshared() {
+    if is_rootless; then
+        $BUILDAH_BINARY unshare "$@"
+    else
+        command "$@"
+    fi
+}
+
+function mkdir() {
+    run_unshared mkdir "$@"
+}
+
+function touch() {
+    run_unshared touch "$@"
+}
+
+function cp() {
+    run_unshared cp "$@"
+}
+
+function rm() {
+    run_unshared rm "$@"
+}
+
 #################
 #  run_buildah  #  Invoke buildah, with timeout, using BATS 'run'
 #################
@@ -195,6 +220,12 @@ function run_buildah() {
     # Remember command args, for possible use in later diagnostic messages
     MOST_RECENT_BUILDAH_COMMAND="buildah $*"
 
+    if is_rootless; then
+        if [[ "$1" =~ mount ]]; then
+            set "unshare" "$BUILDAH_BINARY" ${BUILDAH_REGISTRY_OPTS} ${ROOTDIR_OPTS} "$@"
+        fi
+    fi
+
     while [ $retry -gt 0 ]; do
         retry=$(( retry - 1 ))
```

Now the caveats:
* I've done only cursory testing
* I tried to refactor the `REGISTRY_OPTS` and `ROOTDIR_OPTS` mess, but ended up with spaghettier code. 
* None of this works for the `run_buildah ERRORCODE` tests, because `buildah unshare` has this lovely quirk wherein if a command fails, it loudly tells you `time=... level=error msg="exit status 125"` which of course invalidates output checks. Is there any way to silence those? They don't seem to add any value, since the `buildah unshare` exit status is exactly the same as that of the unshare'd command

@edsantiago Yes `buildah unshare` part complicates this, that is one of the reason I wanted to skip all the tests which needed `buildah unshare` and figure out a better solution for it later since they are few tests. 

@edsantiago Do you have any more concerns other than `buildah unshare` part which needs to be fixed in above PR.
@edsantiago Also we cannot run all rootless tests by default in `unshare` since that will not test if user namespace is created correctly. We should only do `unshare` in tests where its needed.

This is a lot to review; I haven't even finished my first pass.

>We should only do unshare in tests where its needed.

I'm confused. Isn't that what my diff does?

@edsantiago @cevich @nalind  As per discussions from above and before, I have removed `unshare` part from this PR in-order to maintain simplicity for this PR also fixed all the requested nits. Could you PTAL again.
LGTM but I'm curious to know if you reviewed my patch above for fixing the `mount` issue? I believe it's a pretty safe way to increase rootless test coverage.

@edsantiago Your patch looks perfect to me, I'll pick it up in just another followup PR after this, cause we might need a seperate cirrus task for test inside `unshare` so that we can test both `rootless + test_with_unshare` and `rootless + test_without_unshare`.

But I'm fine to do it in this PR as well if you prefer that.

I guess I don't have a strong preference. I was basing my suggestion on @rhatdan's comment

>everyone gets an extra doughnut if they turn on one of the 113 disabled rootless tests.

(in which I would substitute "the satisfaction of increased test coverage" for "doughnut"). I haven't actually looked closely at which tests are disabled, and even if I did I couldn't judge how much we care about those, so I'll leave it up to you. This has my LGTM but I'd like at least one more set of eyes on it.

(And because I just _know_ that someone is going to pipe in with "needs a rebase": No, it doesn't. "branch is out-of-date" does not mean "needs a rebase". Why does the buildah github show those useless messages?)

> Why does the buildah github show those useless messages?

I think it's automatic, but I'll double-check the settings.

@flouthoc nice work, thanks for doing this!
